concepts.title: Conditions for two functions to be identical
concepts.conceptBody:
Identical function 

Two functions $f$ and $g$ are identical if

(i) domain $\text{f}=$ domain $\text{g}$

(ii) $\text{f}\left( \text{n} \right)=\text{g}\left( \text{n} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\forall \text{n}\in $ domain $\text{f}$ or domain $\text{g}$.

Q) Find for what values of $\text{x}$ the following functions are identical 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ f}\left( \text{x} \right)=\text{x}$ and $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)=\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}$ 

(ii) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ f}\left( \text{x} \right)=\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{\text{x}},\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)=\text{x}$ 

Solution : 

(i) Domain of $\text{g}=\left] -\infty ,\infty \right[$ ]

Domain of $\text{f}=\left] -\infty ,\infty \right[$ ]

$g\left( x \right)=\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}}=$ positive square root of ${{x}^{2}}=\left| x \right|=x$, if $x\ge 0$

and $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)=\text{x}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ and $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ are identical $\forall \text{x}\in \left[ 0,\infty \right[$ ]

(ii) Domain of $\text{f}=\text{R}-\left\{ 0 \right\}=\left] -\infty ,0\left[ \cup \right]0,\infty \right[$ ]

Domain of $\text{g}=\left] -\infty ,\infty \right[=\text{R}$

$f\left( x \right)=\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{x}=x$, when $x\ne 0$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ and $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ are identical $\forall \text{x}\ne 0$

examples.title:
Find the values of $x$ for which the following functions are identical.
$f(x)=x$ and $g(x)=\frac{1}{1 / x}$

Find the values of $x$ for which the following functions are identical.
$f(x)=\frac{\sqrt{9-x^2}}{\sqrt{x-2}}$ and $g(x)=\sqrt{\frac{9-x^2}{x-2}}$
Which of the following functions is/are identical to $|x-2|$ ?

Find the values of $x$ for which the following functions are identical.
$f(x)=\cos x \text { and } g(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\tan ^2 x}}$
Identify the pair of functions which are not identical
